agentPrompt: We want to cut log ingest cost. What are the top noisy signals we should drop or throttle?

expected.designNote: Every (ServiceName, SeverityText) cell that contains a noise pattern also contains a high-volume LOAD-BEARING pattern of comparable size. The agent has to inspect event.name / body templates to disambiguate noise from legitimate logs WITHIN each cell — grouping by service+severity alone is insufficient. A blanket drop on any of these cells would destroy load-bearing data.

loadBearing.whyKeep: Push delivery audit trail — required for support and compliance.

noise.remediation: convert to a gauge metric (subscription_mrr); stop emitting as a log

loadBearing.whyKeep: Financial / audit events — required for compliance and revenue recognition.

noise.remediation: downgrade severity or throttle — caught/handled error

loadBearing.whyKeep: Real, unhandled failures — must be kept for incident response.

noise.remediation: drop entirely; replace with a metric

loadBearing.whyKeep: Operational debug used by SREs to diagnose hot-path slowness in each service.

noise.remediation: filter out kube-probe / LB internal health probes at the proxy

loadBearing.whyKeep: Envoy access logs — request-level auditing and debugging.

noise.remediation: stop flushing client-side console.log dumps to backend logs
drop verbose per-query cache-miss logs; replace with a counter

loadBearing.whyKeep: Indexer health signal — needed to track pipeline lag.

criteria.description: Did the answer correctly identify the SEVEN noise patterns and name them by event.name or body template? (1) health.check heartbeats across inventory/pricing/shipping/recommendation services × DEBUG, (2) notification-service cache.hit chatter, (3) billing-service subscription_mrr metric-as-log, (4) worker NonFatalRetryableError caught-exception, (5) frontend-proxy lb.health.probe internal probes, (6) frontend console.log_dump client-side dumps, (7) search-service search.cache.miss verbose. Missing the billing metric-as-log (most subtle — it's INFO not DEBUG) is a correctness failure. Missing pattern 5/6/7 (the new mixed-cell noises) is also a correctness failure.

Did it propose a concrete remediation for each noise pattern (drop, throttle, sample, downgrade severity, or convert to metric)? The billing-service one specifically should be flagged as 'convert to a gauge metric'.

CRITICAL — every (ServiceName, SeverityText) cell that contains a noise pattern ALSO contains a high-volume LOAD-BEARING pattern of comparable size. The agent must DISTINGUISH noise from legitimate within each cell. Specifically: notification-service DEBUG has cache.hit (drop) AND notification.delivery (KEEP, push delivery audit); billing-service INFO has subscription_mrr-as-log (drop/convert) AND invoice/refund/subscription financial events (KEEP); worker ERROR has NonFatalRetryableError (drop/downgrade) AND job.failed.permanent (KEEP, real failures); inventory/pricing/shipping/recommendation × DEBUG each have heartbeats (drop) AND legit per-service ops debug (KEEP); frontend-proxy INFO has lb.health.probe (drop/filter) AND envoy access (KEEP); frontend INFO has console.log_dump (drop) AND page.render (KEEP, RUM); search-service INFO has search.cache.miss (drop) AND index.shard.flush (KEEP). A blanket 'drop notification-service DEBUG' or 'drop worker ERROR' recommendation that does not name the specific event.name to drop is a calibration failure — it would destroy load-bearing data. The recommendation must be event.name-specific, not service-and-severity-specific.
Was the answer concise, well-structured, and ranked clearly?
